rationale: Title Y is stronger for JACC because it states the comparison group and the principal finding. It gives readers the clinical question, the device lead context, and the key safety signal, paravalvular leak, in one title.
loser_weakness: Title X is polished and registry appropriate, but it is more generic. It names outcomes without indicating the main result, so it reads like a descriptive registry report rather than a focused JACC title.
